title: kucoin — Roadmap
text: Version 4.0.0 · Last updated 2026-03-11 98 endpoints implemented across 12 R6 classes. MarketData (16), Trading (21), StopOrders (7), OcoOrders (8), Account (11), Deposit (3), Withdrawal (5), Transfer (2), SubAccount (4), MarginTrading (9), MarginData (5), Lending (7) — complete spot + margin trading scope.

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: Synchronous HTTP blocks R session waiting reply. Asynchronous mode lets fire multiple requests process results arrive — useful bots poll several endpoints place orders parallel.

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: Event loop: R built-event loop like Node.js Python’s asyncio. Promises resolve event loop ticks via later::run_now(). scripts vignettes, drain loop (!loop_empty()) run_now(). Shiny apps event loop runs automatically.
code: box::use(   kucoin[KucoinMarketData, KucoinTrading, KucoinAccount, get_api_keys],   coro[async, await],   later[run_now, loop_empty],   promises[then, catch, promise_all, promise_resolve] )  keys <- get_api_keys(   api_key = "your-api-key",   api_secret = "your-api-secret",   api_passphrase = "your-api-passphrase" )

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: ergonomic way work promises R coro::async, lets write code looks synchronous runs asynchronously hood — just like TypeScript’s async/await. Key pattern: define async function, await API call, return result. Drain event loop (!loop_empty()) run_now().
code: market <- KucoinMarketData$new(async = TRUE) #> Warning: KuCoin API credentials are empty. Set KUCOIN_API_KEY, #> KUCOIN_API_SECRET, and KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE environment variables or pass them #> explicitly. get_stats <- async(function() {   stats <- await(market$get_24hr_stats(symbol = "BTC-USDT"))   return(stats) })  get_stats() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() }

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: Chain several awaited calls sequence — one resolves next begins, just like await TypeScript:
code: results <- NULL  fetch_tickers <- async(function() {   btc <- await(market$get_ticker(symbol = "BTC-USDT"))   eth <- await(market$get_ticker(symbol = "ETH-USDT"))   results <<- list(btc = btc, eth = eth)   return(invisible(NULL)) })  fetch_tickers() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } results$btc results$eth #>                   time      sequence   price       size best_bid best_bid_size #>                 <POSc>        <char>  <char>     <char>   <char>        <char> #> 1: 2024-10-17 10:04:19 1550467636704 67232.9 0.00007682  67232.8    0.41861839 #>    best_ask best_ask_size #>      <char>        <char> #> 1:  67232.9    1.24808993 #>                   time sequence  price   size best_bid best_bid_size best_ask #>                 <POSc>   <char> <char> <char>   <char>        <char>   <char> #> 1: 2024-10-17 10:04:19   200001 2530.6    0.5   2530.5          12.0   2530.8 #>    best_ask_size #>           <char> #> 1:           8.5

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: requests independent, fire simultaneously collect results — async equivalent Promise.() TypeScript:
code: results <- NULL  fetch_parallel <- async(function() {   # Launch both requests concurrently — no await yet, just collect promises   btc_promise <- market$get_ticker(symbol = "BTC-USDT")   eth_promise <- market$get_ticker(symbol = "ETH-USDT")   # Await them together — like Promise.all([btc, eth])   res <- await(promise_all(btc = btc_promise, eth = eth_promise))   results <<- res   return(invisible(NULL)) })  fetch_parallel() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } results$btc results$eth #>                   time      sequence   price       size best_bid best_bid_size #>                 <POSc>        <char>  <char>     <char>   <char>        <char> #> 1: 2024-10-17 10:04:19 1550467636704 67232.9 0.00007682  67232.8    0.41861839 #>    best_ask best_ask_size #>      <char>        <char> #> 1:  67232.9    1.24808993 #>                   time sequence  price   size best_bid best_bid_size best_ask #>                 <POSc>   <char> <char> <char>   <char>        <char>   <char> #> 1: 2024-10-17 10:04:19   200001 2530.6    0.5   2530.5          12.0   2530.8 #>    best_ask_size #>           <char> #> 1:           8.5

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: prefer promise-pipeline style (common JavaScript), use catch:
code: account <- KucoinAccount$new(async = TRUE) #> Warning: KuCoin API credentials are empty. Set KUCOIN_API_KEY, #> KUCOIN_API_SECRET, and KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE environment variables or pass them #> explicitly. chain_result <- NULL  account$get_summary() |>   then(function(summary) {     chain_result <<- summary     return(invisible(NULL))   }) |>   catch(function(err) {     message("Error: ", conditionMessage(err))     return(invisible(NULL))   })  while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } chain_result #>    level sub_quantity max_default_sub_quantity max_sub_quantity #>    <int>        <int>                    <int>            <int> #> 1:     1            3                        5                5 #>    spot_sub_quantity margin_sub_quantity futures_sub_quantity #>                <int>               <int>                <int> #> 1:                 2                   1                    0 #>    option_sub_quantity max_spot_sub_quantity max_margin_sub_quantity #>                  <int>                 <int>                   <int> #> 1:                   0                     5                       5 #>    max_futures_sub_quantity max_option_sub_quantity #>                       <int>                   <int> #> 1:                        5                       5

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: Place test order immediately query open orders — sequential await keeps flow readable:
code: trading <- KucoinTrading$new(async = TRUE) #> Warning: KuCoin API credentials are empty. Set KUCOIN_API_KEY, #> KUCOIN_API_SECRET, and KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE environment variables or pass them #> explicitly. results <- NULL  place_and_check <- async(function() {   # Place a test order   order <- await(trading$add_order_test(     type = "limit",     symbol = "BTC-USDT",     side = "buy",     price = "50000",     size = "0.0001"   ))    # Query open orders   open <- await(trading$get_open_orders(symbol = "BTC-USDT"))    results <<- list(order = order, open = open)   return(invisible(NULL)) })  place_and_check() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() } results$order results$open #>             order_id         client_oid #>               <char>             <char> #> 1: futures-order-001 futures-client-001 #>                          id   symbol op_type   type   side  price   size  funds #>                      <char>   <char>  <char> <char> <char> <char> <char> <char> #> 1: 670fd33bf9406e0007ab3945 BTC-USDT    DEAL  limit    buy  50000 0.0001      0 #>    deal_size deal_funds    fee fee_currency    stp time_in_force cancel_after #>       <char>     <char> <char>       <char> <char>        <char>        <int> #> 1:         0          0      0         USDT                  GTC           -1 #>    post_only hidden iceberg visible_size cancelled_size cancelled_funds #>       <lgcl> <lgcl>  <lgcl>       <char>         <char>          <char> #> 1:     FALSE  FALSE   FALSE            0              0               0 #>    remain_size remain_funds active in_order_book               client_oid #>         <char>       <char> <lgcl>        <lgcl>                   <char> #> 1:      0.0001            0   TRUE          TRUE 5c52e11203aa677f33e493fb #>      tags          created_at     last_updated_at #>    <char>              <POSc>              <POSc> #> 1:        2024-10-22 06:11:55 2024-10-22 06:11:55

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: Inside async functions, can use tryCatch around await calls structured error handling — just like try/catch TypeScript:
code: safe_fetch <- async(function() {   result <- tryCatch(     await(market$get_ticker(symbol = "INVALID-PAIR")),     error = function(e) {       message("Caught error: ", conditionMessage(e))       return(NULL)     }   )   return(result) })  safe_fetch() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() }

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: critical piece async R event loop. Promises resolve event loop ticks. interactive session Shiny app, event loop runs automatically. scripts vignettes, must drive manually. timeout guard: Shiny applications, event loop managed — simply return promises reactive expressions Shiny handles resolution. bots long-running processes, use Scheduler / Looper pattern (see package README) later drives loop automatically.
code: # Idiomatic event loop drain while (!later::loop_empty()) {   later::run_now() } deadline <- lubridate::now() + 30 # 30-second timeout while (!later::loop_empty() && lubridate::now() < deadline) {   later::run_now(timeoutSecs = 0.1) }

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: Rule thumb: await() must appear RHS <- bare statement — never inside another expression. Return values async function extract via () <<- pattern outside async body.

title: Asynchronous Usage with kucoin
text: See vignette("getting-started") full tour spot classes sync mode. See vignette("margin-trading") margin trading, short selling, lending. See vignette("futures-trading") perpetual futures contracts. Browse pkgdown site full method documentation.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Fifteen R6 classes cover spot, futures, margin, lending, sub-account, funding surfaces KuCoin REST API. Every class supports synchronous asynchronous (promise-based) operation via httr2. share common abstract base (KucoinBase) handles authentication, signing, retries, pagination. Standalone (class):

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Set credentials .Renviron: R:
code: KUCOIN_API_KEY=your-api-key KUCOIN_API_SECRET=your-api-secret KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE=your-api-passphrase KUCOIN_API_ENDPOINT=https://api.kucoin.com                   # spot / margin / lending KUCOIN_FUTURES_API_ENDPOINT=https://api-futures.kucoin.com   # futures library(kucoin) keys           <- get_api_keys() market         <- KucoinMarketData$new()                       # public, no keys needed futures_market <- KucoinFuturesMarketData$new()                # public, no keys needed trading        <- KucoinTrading$new(keys = keys) stops          <- KucoinStopOrders$new(keys = keys) oco            <- KucoinOcoOrders$new(keys = keys) futures        <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new(keys = keys) account        <- KucoinAccount$new(keys = keys) deposit        <- KucoinDeposit$new(keys = keys) withdrawal     <- KucoinWithdrawal$new(keys = keys) transfer       <- KucoinTransfer$new(keys = keys) sub            <- KucoinSubAccount$new(keys = keys) futures_acct   <- KucoinFuturesAccount$new(keys = keys) margin_data    <- KucoinMarginData$new(keys = keys) margin         <- KucoinMarginTrading$new(keys = keys) lending        <- KucoinLending$new(keys = keys)

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: kucoin_build_request() constructs signs every request, kucoin_paginate() walks currentPage / pageSize envelope paginated endpoints. rarely call either directly — every method goes — exported can wrap -yet-bound endpoint without re-implementing HMAC-SHA256 signing.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
code: # Backfill 1-hour bars for many symbols, with CSV resume so an aborted # run doesn't restart from scratch. kucoin_backfill_klines(   symbols    = c("BTC-USDT", "ETH-USDT"),   timeframes = c("1day", "1hour"),   from       = lubridate::as_datetime("2024-01-01"),   to         = lubridate::as_datetime("2024-02-01"),   file       = "data/klines.csv" )

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: KuCoin’s APIs use mix millisecond nanosecond UNIX timestamps. two helpers handle :
code: time_convert_to_kucoin(as.POSIXct("2024-01-01", tz = "UTC"), unit = "ms") #> [1] 1704067200000 time_convert_from_kucoin(1704067200000, unit = "ms") #> [1] "2024-01-01 UTC" time_convert_from_kucoin(1704067200000000000, unit = "ns") #> [1] "2024-01-01 UTC"

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
code: verify_symbol("BTC-USDT")   # TRUE verify_symbol("ETH-USDT")   # TRUE verify_symbol("FOOBAR")     # FALSE, with informative message

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Every kucoin method returns nested API data follows one rule: Identify entity endpoint, return one row per entity. trade gets row. order gets row. asset balance gets row. candle gets row. Anything nested entity becomes flat column row additional row different axis — never list column — data dropped; fields don’t fit per-entity row surfaced via sibling method inlined onto existing rows. rule applies sister alpaca binance packages, switching exchanges mean switching mental models data looks. five shape treatments. handful endpoints use two treatments together nesting two layers.

what: Treatment A — ; collapse for arrays of plain strings
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: field array short, plain string values (codes, tags, permissions, announcement types) user mostly filters displays whole list, collapse ; single character column. Filter grepl, recover original vector strsplit(x, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]. package ships shared helper collapse_string_array_fields(x, fields) (NA-safe; emits -per-session warning value contains literal ;). used: get_announcements() ann_type, get_apikey_info()’s permission (KuCoin already returns comma-joined string, recovery idiom strsplit(..., ",")[[1]] rather ";"). separator ; rather , semicolons far less likely appear inside joined values . KuCoin ever ships value contains ;, parser emits -per-session warning catch immediately rather silently corrupting data.
code: ann <- market$get_announcements(page_size = 50, max_pages = 1)  ann$ann_type[1] #> "latest-announcements;new-listings"  # Filter ann[grepl("new-listings", ann_type)]  # Recover the vector strsplit(ann$ann_type[1], ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]] #> "latest-announcements" "new-listings"

what: Treatment B — Long format for arrays of objects
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: array elements records (multiple fields), explode one row per element parent fields replicated. Add 1-indexed position column order matters. used: KucoinSubAccount::get_detail_balance() get_all_spot_balances() (sub-account × wallet × asset), KucoinOcoOrders::get_order_detail_by_id() (sub_order_* legs), KucoinMarketData::get_currency() / get_all_currencies() (chains per currency), KucoinMarginData::get_margin_config() (currency list), KucoinMarginData::get_collateral_ratio() (currencyList × items cross-join), KucoinAccount::get_cross_margin_account() / get_isolated_margin_account() (per-currency / per-pair detail parent fields replicated), orderbook bids / asks. Empty arrays: KuCoin’s Treatment B parsers don’t know every possible child field -front (sub-orders carry stopPrice, others don’t), don’t synthesise NA-filled <noun>_* schema array empty. KucoinOcoOrders::get_order_detail_by_id() sub-orders returns just parent columns; cancel parsers empty cancelledOrderIds return zero-row table. Defensive callers check <noun>_* columns "sub_order_id" %% names(dt) rather assuming ’re always present.
code: # `KucoinSubAccount::get_detail_balance()` returns one row per # (sub-account × wallet bucket × asset). The sub-account-level fields # (`sub_user_id`, `sub_name`, `account_type`) are replicated on each # row. bal <- sub$get_detail_balance(sub_user_id) bal[account_type == "trade", .(currency, balance, available, holds)] # `KucoinOcoOrders::get_order_detail_by_id()` explodes the `orders` # array into `sub_order_*` columns — one row per child leg, parent # fields replicated. detail <- oco$get_order_detail_by_id(order_id) detail[, .(order_id, sub_order_id, sub_order_side, sub_order_price)] # `KucoinAccount::get_cross_margin_account()` returns one row per # currency in the cross-margin wallet, with account-level summary # fields (`total_asset_of_quote_currency`, `debt_ratio`, `status`) # replicated on every row. No sibling method needed for the parent # scalars — they are small enough to inline. ca <- account$get_cross_margin_account(query = list(quoteCurrency = "USDT")) ca[currency == "USDT", .(currency, total, liability, debt_ratio)]

what: Treatment C — Wide prefix for fixed-schema nested objects
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: field single nested object known fixed key set, flatten parent_child columns. used: KucoinAccount::get_isolated_margin_account() baseAsset / quoteAsset. endpoint also uses Treatment B (one row per pair) + replicated parent summary fields, ends three treatments — see column list ?KucoinAccount full schema.
code: # `KucoinAccount::get_isolated_margin_account()` flattens the nested # `baseAsset` / `quoteAsset` per-pair objects into wide columns. iso <- account$get_isolated_margin_account() iso[, .(symbol,         base_asset_currency,  base_asset_borrow_enabled,         quote_asset_currency, quote_asset_borrow_enabled)]

what: Treatment D — Re-route to a sibling method
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: response bundles collection doesn’t fit per-entity row calling endpoint — typically heterogeneous siblings (e.g. account-level summary bundled per-asset rows) — parser keeps rows entity method names, rest exposed via dedicated sibling method. Every method still returns one data.table; data lost. KuCoin uses less frequently binance KuCoin REST API tends namespace different entities different endpoints already. KuCoin bundle, parsers usually prefer Treatment B replicated parent fields (see get_cross_margin_account() ) rather Treatment D, parent scalars small caller doesn’t need second round trip. clearest re-route KuCoin public market data pair -class methods complement rather splitting single response: KucoinMarketData::get_part_orderbook() get_full_orderbook() hit different endpoints, get_full_orderbook() requires authentication. Choose one matches auth state; shape identical.

what: Treatment E — JSON string for dynamic-key or array-of-array objects
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: nested object dynamic keys (different products different key sets) array arrays inner grouping carries semantic meaning, neither ;-collapse wide-prefix preserves structure. Serialise whole field JSON string; recover jsonlite::fromJSON. used: KucoinAccount::get_spot_ledger() get_hf_ledger() context field. endpoints (e.g. get_collateral_ratio()) avoid Treatment E cross-joining nested arrays parser instead.
code: # `KucoinAccount::get_spot_ledger()` and `get_hf_ledger()` preserve the # raw `context` field as a JSON string because its keys depend on # the `bizType` of the entry (Exchange entries carry `orderId` + # `symbol`; Transfer entries carry `description`; etc.). ledger <- account$get_spot_ledger(query = list(currency = "USDT", bizType = "Exchange")) ledger$context[1] #> '{"orderId":"670fd33bf9406e0007ab3945","symbol":"BTC-USDT"}'  # Recover the structure jsonlite::fromJSON(ledger$context[1]) #> $orderId #> [1] "670fd33bf9406e0007ab3945" #> $symbol #> [1] "BTC-USDT"

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: apply every shape treatment: Empty / null array → NA_character_ (list cells). order fills returns fill_id = NA, fill_id = list(). Empty response → empty data.table (synthetic stub rows). KucoinTrading::cancel_all() open orders returns zero-row table, 1-row (symbol, status = "cancelled") fabrication. absence error success signal.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: intentional non-goals, shared sister alpaca binance packages: automatic numeric coercion “string-numeric” fields. KuCoin returns prices, quantities, balances, fees, interest rates fixed-precision strings. package preserves character user controls precision. Cast .numeric() point use. clientOid round-tripped verbatim. Whatever string pass goes back unchanged. normalisation, re-encoding — bring--UUIDs. automatic local-time conversion. Millisecond timestamps come back UTC POSIXct; nanosecond timestamps (trades, order placement times) also UTC POSIXct. Convert format(x, tz = "America/New_York") similar display time. context ledger field stays JSON. Dynamic keys per business type defeat flatten strategy; user calls jsonlite::fromJSON() need metadata. client-side rate-limiting. package surfaces KuCoin’s rate-limit error codes back — caller’s job. reconnect / retry transient network errors. single call asked ; wrap httr2::req_retry() (passed kucoin_build_request()) want retries.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: alpaca, binance, kucoin packages follow shape rule. means ’ve learned data.table idioms one , idioms work others — pivot portfolio’s balances across multiple wallets exchanges straight rbindlist plus exchange / wallet column, per-source shape massage. idiom works orders: …without escape hatches lapply hidden lists.
code: # Combine spot + futures + cross-margin balances into one ledger ledger <- data.table::rbindlist(   list(     account$get_spot_accounts()[, wallet := "spot"],     futures_acct$get_account_overview("USDT")[, wallet := "futures"],     account$get_cross_margin_account()[, wallet := "margin"]   ),   use.names = TRUE,   fill = TRUE ) ledger[as.numeric(available) > 0] # Top 5 highest-priced asks in the book depth[side == "ask"][order(-price)][1:5]  # All BTC-USDT orders that filled at > 50k orders <- trading$get_closed_orders(symbol = "BTC-USDT") orders[as.numeric(deal_funds) > 50000]

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: vignette("getting-started", package = "kucoin") — package tour. vignette("async-usage", package = "kucoin") — using methods async = TRUE. vignette("futures-trading", package = "kucoin") vignette("margin-trading", package = "kucoin") — surface-specific walkthroughs. sister alpaca binance packages — convention applied different exchanges. binance’s vignette("data-shapes") Package Tour layout 13 R6 classes.

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
text: package provides three classes futures operations: futures classes use separate base URL (https://api-futures.kucoin.com) share API key credentials spot trading.

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
code: box::use(   kucoin[     KucoinFuturesMarketData, KucoinFuturesTrading, KucoinFuturesAccount,     get_api_keys   ] )  keys <- get_api_keys(   api_key = "your-api-key",   api_secret = "your-api-secret",   api_passphrase = "your-api-passphrase" )  market <- KucoinFuturesMarketData$new(keys = keys) trading <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new(keys = keys) account <- KucoinFuturesAccount$new(keys = keys)

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
text: Real-time price best bid/ask single contract:

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
text: Perpetual futures settle funding every 8 hours. Positive rates mean longs pay shorts; negative means shorts pay longs:
code: rate <- market$get_funding_rate(symbol = "XBTUSDTM") rate #>      symbol granularity          time_point value predicted_value #>      <char>       <int>              <POSc> <num>           <num> #> 1: XBTUSDTM    28800000 2024-10-17 08:00:00 1e-04           1e-04 #>           funding_time #>                 <POSc> #> 1: 2024-10-17 16:00:00

code: market$get_server_time() market$get_service_status() #>            server_time #>                 <POSc> #> 1: 2024-10-17 10:04:19 #>    status    msg #>    <char> <char> #> 1:   open

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
text: trading methods require API keys Futures trading permission enabled KuCoin.

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
text: Use add_order_test() validate parameters without placing real order:
code: result <- trading$add_order_test(   clientOid = "my-test-001",   symbol = "XBTUSDTM",   side = "buy",   type = "limit",   leverage = 5,   size = 1,   price = "98000" ) result #>             order_id         client_oid #>               <char>             <char> #> 1: futures-order-001 futures-client-001

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
code: # Market order — no price needed order <- trading$add_order(   clientOid = "my-market-001",   symbol = "XBTUSDTM",   side = "buy",   type = "market",   leverage = 10,   size = 1 )  # Limit order order <- trading$add_order(   clientOid = "my-limit-001",   symbol = "XBTUSDTM",   side = "buy",   type = "limit",   leverage = 5,   size = 1,   price = "95000",   timeInForce = "GTC" )

title: Futures Trading with kucoin
code: # Cancel by system order ID trading$cancel_order_by_id(orderId = "order-id-here")  # Cancel by client order ID trading$cancel_order_by_client_oid(clientOid = "my-limit-001", symbol = "XBTUSDTM")  # Cancel all orders for a symbol trading$cancel_all(symbol = "XBTUSDTM")  # Cancel all stop orders trading$cancel_all_stop_orders(symbol = "XBTUSDTM")

code: # Get a specific order order <- trading$get_order_by_id(orderId = "order-id")  # Get open orders open <- trading$get_order_list(query = list(status = "active"))  # Get recent closed orders closed <- trading$get_recent_closed_orders(symbol = "XBTUSDTM")

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: KucoinTrading class manages HF spot orders. trading endpoints require authentication.

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: Test orders validate parameters without executing:
code: trading <- KucoinTrading$new() #> Warning: KuCoin API credentials are empty. Set KUCOIN_API_KEY, #> KUCOIN_API_SECRET, and KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE environment variables or pass them #> explicitly. result <- trading$add_order_test(   type = "limit",   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   side = "buy",   price = "50000",   size = "0.0001" ) result #>             order_id         client_oid #>               <char>             <char> #> 1: futures-order-001 futures-client-001

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: Sync variants return fill results single round trip:
code: # Place order and get immediate fill result order <- trading$add_order_sync(   type = "limit", symbol = "BTC-USDT", side = "buy",   price = "50000", size = "0.0001" ) cat("Status:", order$status, "Filled:", order$deal_size, "\n")  # Modify an existing order's price modified <- trading$modify_order(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   orderId = order$order_id,   newPrice = "51000" ) cat("New order ID:", modified$new_order_id, "\n")

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: dead-man’s switch auto-cancels orders bot stops heartbeating:
code: # Enable: cancel all BTC-USDT orders if no request in 30 seconds trading$set_dcp(timeout = 30, symbols = "BTC-USDT")  # Check current DCP settings dcp <- trading$get_dcp() cat("Timeout:", dcp$timeout, "s\n")  # Disable trading$set_dcp(timeout = -1)

title: Getting Started with kucoin
code: # Base fee rate (account tier default) base_fees <- account$get_base_fee_rate() cat("Base taker:", base_fees$taker_fee_rate, "\n")  # Per-symbol actual rates (after VIP/KCS discounts) actual_fees <- account$get_fee_rate(symbols = "BTC-USDT,ETH-USDT") actual_fees

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: HF ledger contains fills, fees, settlements HF orders (7-day window):

title: Getting Started with kucoin
code: # Check clock drift st <- market$get_server_time() drift_ms <- as.numeric(lubridate::now()) * 1000 - st$server_time cat("Clock drift:", round(drift_ms), "ms\n")  # Pre-flight: is the exchange operational? status <- market$get_service_status() if (status$status != "open") stop("Exchange not operational: ", status$msg)

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: Deposits land main account, HF spot orders require funds trade account. Use KucoinTransfer move funds account types.
code: box::use(kucoin[KucoinTransfer])  transfer <- KucoinTransfer$new()  # Check how much USDT is available to transfer balance <- transfer$get_transferable(currency = "USDT", type = "MAIN") balance  # Transfer USDT from main to trade account result <- transfer$add_transfer(   clientOid = "unique-id-here",   currency = "USDT",   amount = "100",   type = "INTERNAL",   fromAccountType = "MAIN",   toAccountType = "TRADE" ) result

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: Use KucoinWithdrawal check withdrawal limits, initiate withdrawals, monitor withdrawal status.
code: box::use(kucoin[KucoinWithdrawal])  withdrawal <- KucoinWithdrawal$new()  # Check withdrawal quotas and fees quotas <- withdrawal$get_withdrawal_quotas(currency = "USDT", chain = "trx") quotas  # Withdraw USDT via TRC20 result <- withdrawal$add_withdrawal(   currency = "USDT",   toAddress = "your-trc20-address",   amount = "10",   withdrawType = "ADDRESS",   chain = "trx" ) result  # Check withdrawal history history <- withdrawal$get_withdrawal_history(currency = "USDT") history

Set KUCOIN_API_KEY, #> KUCOIN_API_SECRET, and KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE environment variables or pass them #> explicitly. subs <- sub$get_sub_account_list() subs #>                     user_id       uid  sub_name status  type  access #>                      <char>     <int>    <char>  <int> <int>  <char> #> 1: 641e7f09df0db80001f1e5ac 169630809 bot-alpha      2     0    Spot #> 2: 641e8027df0db80001f1e6bb 169630810  bot-beta      2     0 Futures #>        remarks          created_at #>         <char>              <POSc> #> 1: Trading bot 2024-10-17 10:04:19 #> 2: Futures bot 2024-10-17 10:05:59

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: default, HMAC request signatures use local machine’s clock. system time sync KuCoin’s servers, authenticated requests may fail. can configure class fetch server time authenticated request: time_source parameter available class constructors: KucoinTrading, KucoinAccount, KucoinStopOrders, KucoinOcoOrders, KucoinDeposit, KucoinTransfer, KucoinWithdrawal, KucoinSubAccount.
code: # Use server time for signing (adds one round trip per request) trading <- KucoinTrading$new(time_source = "server")  # All authenticated calls now use the exchange clock order <- trading$add_order_test(   type = "limit", symbol = "BTC-USDT", side = "buy",   price = "50000", size = "0.0001" )

title: Getting Started with kucoin
text: See vignette("async-usage") promise-based asynchronous operation. See vignette("margin-trading") margin trading, short selling, lending. See vignette("futures-trading") perpetual futures contracts. Browse pkgdown site full method documentation. bulk historical data downloads, see ?kucoin_backfill_klines.

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: package provides three classes margin operations:

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
code: box::use(   kucoin[     KucoinMarginTrading, KucoinMarginData, KucoinLending, get_api_keys   ] )  keys <- get_api_keys(   api_key = "your-api-key",   api_secret = "your-api-secret",   api_passphrase = "your-api-passphrase" )  margin <- KucoinMarginTrading$new(keys = keys) margin_data <- KucoinMarginData$new(keys = keys) lending <- KucoinLending$new(keys = keys)

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: Short selling lets profit price decline. package uses intent-based methods handle borrowing repayment automatically:
code: # Open a short: borrows BTC and sells it order <- margin$open_short(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001 ) order  # Later, close the short: buys BTC back and repays the loan close <- margin$close_short(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001 ) close #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001 #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: leveraged long lets buy asset balance allows:
code: # Open a leveraged long: borrows USDT and buys BTC order <- margin$open_long(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001 ) order  # Later, close the long: sells BTC and repays the USDT loan close <- margin$close_long(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001 ) close #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001 #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: four methods default market orders. Use type = "limit" limit orders:
code: order <- margin$open_short(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   type = "limit",   price = 100000,   size = 0.001,   timeInForce = "GTC" ) order #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: default, methods use cross margin (shared collateral pool). isolated margin (risk limited one pair), set isIsolated = TRUE:
code: order <- margin$open_short(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001,   isIsolated = TRUE ) order #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: Validate parameters without placing real order:
code: test <- margin$open_short(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001,   dry_run = TRUE ) test #>                    order_id       client_oid borrow_size  loan_apply_id #>                      <char>           <char>      <char>         <char> #> 1: 6789abcd1234ef0007ab1234 margin-order-001       0.001 loan-apply-001

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: Track orders identifiers:
code: order <- margin$open_short(   symbol = "BTC-USDT",   size = 0.001,   clientOid = "my-strategy-short-001" ) order$client_oid #> [1] "margin-order-001"

title: Margin Trading with kucoin
text: intent-based methods (open_short, close_short, etc.) handle borrowing repayment automatically. advanced workflows want explicit control:
code: # Manually borrow USDT loan <- margin$borrow(currency = "USDT", size = 1000) loan  # ... do your trading ...  # Manually repay result <- margin$repay(currency = "USDT", size = 1000) result #>            order_no actual_size #>              <char>      <char> #> 1: borrow-order-001        1000 #>           order_no actual_size           timestamp #>             <char>      <char>              <POSc> #> 1: repay-order-001        1000 2024-10-23 03:53:26

Lend 1000 USDT at 5% interest order <- lending$purchase(currency = "USDT", size = 1000, interestRate = 0.05) order$order_no  # Update the interest rate lending$modify_purchase(   currency = "USDT",   purchaseOrderNo = order$order_no,   interestRate = 0.06 )  # Check your lending orders orders <- lending$get_purchase_orders(query = list(currency = "USDT", status = "DONE")) orders  # Redeem what: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
title: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
text: R API wrapper KuCoin cryptocurrency exchange. Provides R6 classes spot market data, trading, stop orders, OCO orders, account management, deposits, transfers, withdrawals, sub-accounts, margin trading, margin lending, futures trading. Supports synchronous asynchronous (promise based) operation via httr2.

title: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
text: software provided “”, without warranty kind. package interacts live cryptocurrency exchange accounts can execute real trades, transfers, withdrawals involving real money. using package accept full responsibility financial losses, erroneous transactions, damages may result. Always test small amounts first, use API key permissions restrict access need, never share API credentials. author(s) contributor(s) liable financial loss damage arising use software. invite read source code make contributions find bug wish make improvement.

title: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
text: API responses returned data.table objects three transformations applied: snake_case column names — camelCase keys JSON response (e.g. clientOid, orderType, createdAt) become snake_case (client_oid, order_type, created_at). handful endpoints additionally reshape nested objects wide parent_child columns (e.g. baseAsset.currency → base_asset_currency) collapse array fields plural form. See method’s @return exact column list. Type coercion well-known columns — KuCoin’s millisecond timestamps (endpoints) nanosecond timestamps (futures orderbooks / klines) parsed POSIXct UTC. Numeric quantities, prices, ratios stay character strings KuCoin emits strings precision matters; cast .numeric() point use need arithmetic. One entity = one row, list columns — every method follows rule “identify entity endpoint, return one row per entity”. convention shared sister alpaca binance packages switching exchanges doesn’t mean switching mental models. five shape treatments parsers apply, depending nested structure: Two cross-cutting rules apply every shape treatment: Empty / null array → NA_character_ (list cells). OCO order children returns sub_order_id = NA, sub_order_id = list(). Empty response → empty data.table (synthetic stub rows). KucoinTrading$cancel_all() open orders returns zero-row table, fabricated (symbol, status = "cancelled") placeholder. absence error success signal. full per-treatment catalogue worked examples, see vignette("data-shapes", package = "kucoin").

title: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
text: Set API credentials environment variables .Renviron: don’t key, visit KuCoin API documentation.
code: # special mock for local build box::use(   kucoin[     get_api_keys,     get_futures_base_url   ],   ./tests/testthat/mock_router[mock_router] )  KEYS <- get_api_keys(   api_key = "fake-key",   api_secret = "fake-secret",   api_passphrase = "fake-passphrase" )  BASE <- "https://api.kucoin.com" FBASE <- "https://api-futures.kucoin.com"  options(httr2_mock = mock_router)  # normal imports box::use(   kucoin[     KucoinMarketData,     KucoinTrading,     KucoinAccount,     KucoinMarginTrading,     KucoinMarginData,     KucoinLending,     KucoinFuturesMarketData,     KucoinFuturesTrading,     KucoinFuturesAccount   ],   lubridate[ymd_hms] ) KUCOIN_API_ENDPOINT = "https://api.kucoin.com" KUCOIN_API_KEY = your-api-key KUCOIN_API_SECRET = your-api-secret KUCOIN_API_PASSPHRASE = your-api-passphrase

title: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
text: Essential trading bots: deposits land main account, HF spot orders require funds trade account.
code: transfer <- KucoinTransfer$new()  # Check transferable balance balance <- transfer$get_transferable(currency = "USDT", type = "MAIN") print(balance[, .(currency, balance, transferable)])  # Move funds from main to trade account result <- transfer$add_transfer(   clientOid = "my-unique-id",   currency = "USDT",   amount = "100",   type = "INTERNAL",   fromAccountType = "MAIN",   toAccountType = "TRADE" ) print(result$order_id)  # Check withdrawal quotas withdrawal <- KucoinWithdrawal$new() quotas <- withdrawal$get_withdrawal_quotas(currency = "USDT", chain = "trx") print(quotas[, .(currency, available_amount, withdraw_min_fee)])

title: API Wrapper to KuCoin Cryptocurrency Exchange
text: package meant used asynchronous non-blocking event loop (.e. à la JavaScript) written around promises. Please use later run event loop. recommend pattern shown . offer synchronous asynchronous instance classes. classes accept async = TRUE, makes methods return promises instead objects. can resolve promises whichever way like, either $() chaining async/await patterns. recommend use coro::async() write sequential looking async code:
code: box::use(coro, later)  market_async <- KucoinMarketData$new(keys = KEYS, base_url = BASE, async = TRUE)  main <- coro$async(function() {   ticker <- await(market_async$get_ticker(symbol = "BTC-USDT"))   klines <- await(market_async$get_klines(symbol = "BTC-USDT", timeframe = "1hour"))    print(ticker)   print(klines) })  main()  while (!later$loop_empty()) {   later$run_now() } #>                   time      sequence   price       size best_bid best_bid_size #>                 <POSc>        <char>  <char>     <char>   <char>        <char> #> 1: 2024-10-17 10:04:19 1550467636704 67232.9 0.00007682  67232.8    0.41861839 #>    best_ask best_ask_size #>      <char>        <char> #> 1:  67232.9    1.24808993 #>               datetime     open     high      low    close   volume turnover #>                 <POSc>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num> #> 1: 2025-07-26 12:00:00 117775.9 118221.2 117766.4 118128.9 264.6461 31241540 #> 2: 2025-07-26 16:00:00 118129.0 118291.8 117940.3 118227.4 197.8112 23355797 #> 3: 2025-07-26 20:00:00 118227.3 118299.3 117880.4 117915.0 252.9352 29854685

text: Provides methods querying account information, balances, ledger history KuCoin. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinAccount: Account and Funding Management — KucoinAccount
text: Account Summary: Retrieve VIP level, sub-account count, general account metadata. API Key Inspection: Query permissions, IP whitelist, expiry active API key. Spot Accounts: List spot/margin/trade accounts balances, inspect single account ID. Margin Accounts: Retrieve cross-margin isolated-margin account details including liability asset info. Ledger History: Paginated transaction history across spot margin accounts datetime conversion.

title: KucoinAccount: Account and Funding Management — KucoinAccount
text: methods require authentication (valid API key, secret, passphrase set via environment variables passed constructor). class supports synchronous asynchronous (coro/promises) operation modes inherited KucoinBase.
code: # Synchronous usage account <- KucoinAccount$new() summary <- account$get_summary() print(summary)  # Asynchronous usage account_async <- KucoinAccount$new(async = TRUE) main <- coro::async(function() {   summary <- await(account_async$get_summary())   print(summary) }) main() while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now()

text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) one row per currency (Treatment B). account-level summary fields (total_asset_of_quote_currency, total_liability_of_quote_currency, debt_ratio, status) replicated every row caller never needs sibling method. Columns (subset; exact set depends KuCoin payload): currency (character): Currency code (e.g. "USDT", "BTC"). total (character): Total balance. available (character): Available balance. hold (character): Amount hold. liability (character): Total liability. liability_principal (character): Liability principal. liability_interest (character): Liability interest. max_borrow_size (character): Maximum borrowable amount. borrow_enabled (logical): Whether borrowing enabled. transfer_in_enabled (logical): Whether transfer-enabled. total_asset_of_quote_currency (character): Account-level total asset (repeated). total_liability_of_quote_currency (character): Account-level total liability (repeated). debt_ratio (character): Account-level debt ratio (repeated). status (character): Account-level status (repeated). Returns empty data.table margin accounts exist.

title: KucoinAccount: Account and Funding Management — KucoinAccount
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) one row per isolated-margin pair (Treatment B) nested baseAsset / quoteAsset objects flattened wide-prefix (Treatment C). account-level fields total_asset_of_quote_currency, total_liability_of_quote_currency, timestamp replicated every row. Columns: symbol (character): Isolated pair symbol (e.g. "BTC-USDT"). status (character): Account status (e.g. "EFFECTIVE"). debt_ratio (character): Liability--asset ratio. base_asset_currency (character): Base asset code. base_asset_borrow_enabled (logical): Whether borrowing base asset allowed. base_asset_transfer_in_enabled (logical): Whether transferring base asset allowed. base_asset_liability (character): Total base-asset liability. base_asset_liability_principal (character): Base-asset liability principal. base_asset_liability_interest (character): Base-asset liability interest. base_asset_total (character): Base-asset total balance. base_asset_available (character): Base-asset available balance. base_asset_hold (character): Base-asset amount hold. base_asset_max_borrow_size (character): Base-asset maximum borrowable. quote_asset_currency (character): Quote asset code. quote_asset_borrow_enabled (logical): Whether borrowing quote asset allowed. quote_asset_transfer_in_enabled (logical): Whether transferring quote asset allowed. quote_asset_liability (character): Total quote-asset liability. quote_asset_liability_principal (character): Quote-asset liability principal. quote_asset_liability_interest (character): Quote-asset liability interest. quote_asset_total (character): Quote-asset total balance. quote_asset_available (character): Quote-asset available balance. quote_asset_hold (character): Quote-asset amount hold. quote_asset_max_borrow_size (character): Quote-asset maximum borrowable. total_asset_of_quote_currency (character): Account-level total asset (repeated). total_liability_of_quote_currency (character): Account-level total liability (repeated). timestamp (POSIXct): Snapshot timestamp (repeated). Returns empty data.table isolated-margin pairs exist.

text: query Named list; optional filter parameters. Supported keys: currency (character): Filter currency code e.g. "USDT", "BTC". direction (character): Filter direction: "" "". bizType (character): Business type filter e.g. "Exchange", "Deposit", "Withdrawal", "Transfer", "Trade_Exchange". startAt (numeric): Start time milliseconds (epoch). Inclusive. endAt (numeric): End time milliseconds (epoch). Inclusive. page_size Integer; number results per page, 10 500. Default 50. max_pages Numeric; maximum number pages fetch. Default Inf (fetch pages). Set finite number limit API calls.

title: KucoinAccount: Account and Funding Management — KucoinAccount
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) columns: id (character): Ledger entry ID. currency (character): Currency code. amount (character): Transaction amount. fee (character): Fee charged. balance (character): Balance transaction. account_type (character): Account type e.g. "TRADE", "MAIN". biz_type (character): Business type. direction (character): "" "". context (character): JSON metadata. created_at (POSIXct): Coerced epoch milliseconds. Returns empty data.table ledger entries match.

title: KucoinAccount: Account and Funding Management — KucoinAccount
code: account <- KucoinAccount$new()  # Get recent USDT trade ledger entries ledger <- account$get_spot_ledger(   query = list(currency = "USDT", bizType = "Exchange"),   page_size = 100,   max_pages = 5 ) print(ledger)  # Get all ledger entries for the last 24 hours now_ms <- as.numeric(lubridate::now()) * 1000 ledger_24h <- account$get_spot_ledger(   query = list(startAt = now_ms - 86400000, endAt = now_ms) ) print(ledger_24h[, .(currency, amount, direction, created_at)])

------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_spot_account_detail()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new()  # First get all accounts to find the ID accounts <- account$get_spot_accounts(query = list(currency = "USDT", type = "trade")) account_id <- accounts$id[1]  # Then query the specific account detail <- account$get_spot_account_detail(account_id) cat("Balance:", detail$balance, "Available:", detail$available, "\\n") } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_cross_margin_account()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new() margin <- account$get_cross_margin_account(query = list(quoteCurrency = "USDT")) print(margin) # Check debt ratio cat("Liabilities:", margin[currency == "USDT", liability], "\\n") } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_isolated_margin_account()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new() isolated <- account$get_isolated_margin_account(   query = list(symbol = "BTC-USDT", quoteCurrency = "USDT") ) print(isolated) } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_spot_ledger()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new()  # Get recent USDT trade ledger entries ledger <- account$get_spot_ledger(   query = list(currency = "USDT", bizType = "Exchange"),   page_size = 100,   max_pages = 5 ) print(ledger)  # Get all ledger entries for the last 24 hours now_ms <- as.numeric(lubridate::now()) * 1000 ledger_24h <- account$get_spot_ledger(   query = list(startAt = now_ms - 86400000, endAt = now_ms) ) print(ledger_24h[, .(currency, amount, direction, created_at)]) } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_hf_ledger()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new() hf <- account$get_hf_ledger(currency = "USDT", bizType = "TRADE_EXCHANGE") print(hf[, .(currency, amount, fee, direction, created_at)]) } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_base_fee_rate()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new() fees <- account$get_base_fee_rate() cat("Taker:", fees$taker_fee_rate, "Maker:", fees$maker_fee_rate, "\n") } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinAccount$get_fee_rate()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ account <- KucoinAccount$new() fees <- account$get_fee_rate("BTC-USDT,ETH-USDT") print(fees[, .(symbol, taker_fee_rate, maker_fee_rate)]) } # }

what: KucoinBase: Abstract Base Class for KuCoin API Clients — KucoinBase
title: KucoinBase: Abstract Base Class for KuCoin API Clients — KucoinBase
text: Provides shared infrastructure KuCoin R6 classes, including API credential management, sync/async execution mode, timestamp source configuration, standardised method calling implementation functions.

title: KucoinBase: Abstract Base Class for KuCoin API Clients — KucoinBase
text: class thin KuCoin specialisation connectcore::RestClient, shared transport base. Credential storage, sync/async mode flag, server-time clock source, is_async / time_source active bindings live connectcore; KucoinBase supplies two venue-specific seams — KuCoin authenticates request (.sign(), adds header-based HMAC signature, encrypted passphrase, KC-API-* headers) reports error (.parse_envelope(), honours KuCoin's code/data envelope). Unlike connectors, KuCoin signs exact compact JSON request body must send byte sequence wire, request funnel KuCoin's kucoin_build_request() (signs sends identical req_body_raw payload) rather connectcore default funnel; two overridable seams driven .

title: KucoinBase: Abstract Base Class for KuCoin API Clients — KucoinBase
text: async parameter controls execution mode API methods: async = FALSE (default): methods return results directly (data.table, character, etc.). async = TRUE: methods return promises::promise objects resolve types. async, use coro::async() await() promises::() consume results. promises package must installed async mode (Suggests dependency).

title: KucoinBase: Abstract Base Class for KuCoin API Clients — KucoinBase
text: time_source parameter controls clock used HMAC request signing: "local" (default): uses local UTC clock. "server": fetches KuCoin server time via GET /api/v1/timestamp authenticated request. slower (one extra HTTP round trip) ensures signing works even local clock sync.

title: KucoinBase: Abstract Base Class for KuCoin API Clients — KucoinBase
text: class meant instantiated directly. Subclasses (e.g., KucoinMarketData, KucoinTrading) inherit define public methods delegate private$.request() private$.paginate().

title: KucoinFuturesMarketData: Futures Market Data Retrieval — KucoinFuturesMarketData
text: symbol Character; futures symbol (e.g., "XBTUSDTM"). granularity Integer; candle interval minutes. Supported values: 1, 5, 15, 30, 60, 120, 240, 480, 720, 1440, 10080. POSIXct, numeric, NULL; start time. POSIXct, converted milliseconds. numeric, assumed milliseconds. POSIXct, numeric, NULL; end time. fetch_all Logical; TRUE, automatically segments time range multiple API calls 200 candles , fetches segments, deduplicates overlapping boundaries, returns combined result sorted datetime. required enabled. Warning: large date ranges consume multiple API requests may impact rate-limit quota. Default FALSE. sleep Numeric; seconds wait consecutive API calls fetch_all = TRUE. Use avoid hitting KuCoin rate limits. applies synchronous mode; async mode chains requests sequentially via promises. Default 0.2.

title: KucoinFuturesMarketData: Futures Market Data Retrieval — KucoinFuturesMarketData
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) columns: datetime (POSIXct): Candle open time (coerced milliseconds). open (numeric): Opening price. high (numeric): Highest price. low (numeric): Lowest price. close (numeric): Closing price. volume (numeric): Trading volume contracts. turnover (numeric): Trading turnover settlement currency.

title: KucoinFuturesMarketData: Futures Market Data Retrieval — KucoinFuturesMarketData
code: futures_market <- KucoinFuturesMarketData$new()  # Single call: last 200 hourly candles klines <- futures_market$get_klines(   "XBTUSDTM",   granularity = 60,   from = as.numeric(Sys.time() - 200 * 3600) * 1000,   to = as.numeric(Sys.time()) * 1000 ) print(klines[, .(datetime, open, high, low, close, volume)])  # Fetch all: complete hourly history for a date range all_klines <- futures_market$get_klines(   "XBTUSDTM",   granularity = 60,   from = as.POSIXct("2024-10-01", tz = "UTC"),   to = as.POSIXct("2024-10-31", tz = "UTC"),   fetch_all = TRUE,   sleep = 0.3 ) print(nrow(all_klines))

title: KucoinFuturesTrading: Futures Order Management — KucoinFuturesTrading
text: Provides methods placing, cancelling, querying futures orders KuCoin. Supports limit market orders, stop orders, batch operations, Dead Connection Protection (DCP). Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinFuturesTrading: Futures Order Management — KucoinFuturesTrading
text: Order Placement: Place single batch limit/market futures orders configurable leverage, margin mode, position side. Order Cancellation: Cancel individual orders system ID client OID, cancel open/stop orders . Order Queries: Retrieve order details, paginated order lists, recent closed orders, stop orders. Trade History: Query fill records (paginated recent) fee liquidity details. Risk Management: Configure Dead Connection Protection (DCP) auto-cancel orders connectivity loss.

------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinFuturesTrading$get_stop_orders()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ft <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new()  # Get all untriggered stop orders for XBTUSDTM stops <- ft$get_stop_orders(query = list(symbol = "XBTUSDTM")) print(stops[, .(id, side, stop_price, price, status)])  # Get all untriggered stop orders all_stops <- ft$get_stop_orders() } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinFuturesTrading$get_fills()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ft <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new()  # Get all fills for XBTUSDTM fills <- ft$get_fills(query = list(symbol = "XBTUSDTM")) print(fills[, .(trade_id, side, price, size, fee, trade_time)])  # Get fills for a specific order order_fills <- ft$get_fills(query = list(orderId = "234125150956625920")) } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinFuturesTrading$get_recent_fills()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ft <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new()  # Get recent fills for XBTUSDTM recent <- ft$get_recent_fills(symbol = "XBTUSDTM") print(recent[, .(trade_id, side, price, size, fee, trade_time)])  # Get all recent fills all_recent <- ft$get_recent_fills() } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinFuturesTrading$get_open_order_value()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ft <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new() stats <- ft$get_open_order_value(symbol = "XBTUSDTM") print(stats[, .(open_order_buy_qty, open_order_sell_qty, settle_currency)]) } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinFuturesTrading$set_dcp()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ft <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new()  # Enable DCP with 60-second timeout for XBTUSDTM result <- ft$set_dcp(timeout = 60, symbol = "XBTUSDTM") print(result)  # Disable DCP ft$set_dcp(timeout = -1) } # }  ## ------------------------------------------------ ## Method `KucoinFuturesTrading$get_dcp()` ## ------------------------------------------------  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ft <- KucoinFuturesTrading$new()  # Check DCP settings for XBTUSDTM dcp <- ft$get_dcp(symbol = "XBTUSDTM") print(dcp[, .(timeout, symbols)])  # Check global DCP settings dcp_global <- ft$get_dcp() } # }

title: KucoinLending: Margin Lending Operations — KucoinLending
text: Provides methods lending assets KuCoin margin lending market, managing purchase (lend) orders, redeeming lent assets. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinLending: Margin Lending Operations — KucoinLending
text: Loan Market Data: Query available lending currencies historical interest rates. Purchase (Lend): Lend assets margin pool earn interest. Modify: Update interest rate existing lending orders. Redeem: Withdraw lent assets lending pool. Order Queries: Retrieve purchase redemption order history.

title: KucoinMarginData: Margin Market Information — KucoinMarginData
text: Provides methods querying margin-specific market data including supported symbols, configuration, risk limits, collateral ratios. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinMarginData: Margin Market Information — KucoinMarginData
text: Cross Margin Symbols: Query symbols available cross margin trading. Isolated Margin Symbols: Query symbols available isolated margin trading. Margin Config: Retrieve global margin configuration (max leverage, liquidation ratios). Collateral Ratios: Query collateral ratio tiers currency. Risk Limits: Query borrow/hold limits per currency symbol.

title: KucoinMarginData: Margin Market Information — KucoinMarginData
text: methods public (auth required). get_risk_limit() requires authentication General permission.

title: KucoinMarginData: Margin Market Information — KucoinMarginData
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) one row per cross-margin symbol following columns (subset shown — KuCoin may add fields): symbol (character): Trading pair identifier (e.g. "BTC-USDT"). name (character): Display name. enable_trading (logical): Whether trading enabled. market (character): Market category (e.g. "USDS"). base_currency (character): Base asset code. quote_currency (character): Quote asset code. base_increment (character): Minimum base-quantity step. base_min_size (character): Minimum order base quantity. base_max_size (character): Maximum order base quantity. quote_increment (character): Minimum quote-quantity step. quote_min_size (character): Minimum order quote quantity. quote_max_size (character): Maximum order quote quantity. price_increment (character): Minimum price step. fee_currency (character): Currency charged trading fees. price_limit_rate (character): Maximum allowed price deviation. min_funds (character): Minimum order notional. Empty response yields empty data.table.

text: KuCoin Get Isolated Margin Symbols Verified: 2026-05-23

title: KucoinMarginData: Margin Market Information — KucoinMarginData
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) one row per isolated-margin pair columns: symbol (character): Trading pair identifier. symbol_name (character): Display name. base_currency (character): Base asset code. quote_currency (character): Quote asset code. max_leverage (integer): Maximum leverage available. fl_debt_ratio (character): Forced-liquidation debt ratio. trade_enable (logical): Whether trading enabled. base_borrow_enable (logical): Base-currency borrow allowed. quote_borrow_enable (logical): Quote-currency borrow allowed. base_transfer_in_enable (logical): Base-currency transfer-allowed. quote_transfer_in_enable (logical): Quote-currency transfer-allowed. title: KucoinMarginData: Margin Market Information — KucoinMarginData
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) one row per (currency, tier) pair. nested currencyList/items arrays cross-joined flat long table. what: KucoinMarginTrading: Margin Order and Debit Management — KucoinMarginTrading
title: KucoinMarginTrading: Margin Order and Debit Management — KucoinMarginTrading
text: Provides intent-based methods margin trading KuCoin. Instead raw side = "buy" / side = "sell" parameters (ambiguous margin context), class exposes four clear actions: open_long(): Buy asset borrowed funds (leveraged long). close_long(): Sell previously-bought asset repay loan. open_short(): Borrow sell asset (short sell). close_short(): Buy back previously-shorted asset repay loan. Additionally provides methods manual borrowing, repaying, interest queries, leverage configuration. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinMarginTrading: Margin Order and Debit Management — KucoinMarginTrading
text: open_short() borrows base asset (e.g. BTC) immediately sells market price. now hold proceeds (e.g. USDT) owe borrowed BTC plus interest. price drops, close_short() buys back asset lower price automatically repays loan. difference profit. price rises instead, lose money — margin trading carries liquidation risk.

title: KucoinMarginTrading: Margin Order and Debit Management — KucoinMarginTrading
text: Cross margin (default): margin positions share collateral pool. loss one pair can offset gains another, liquidation affects entire margin account. Isolated margin (isIsolated = TRUE): trading pair collateral. Losses contained pair, need allocate collateral per pair.

title: KucoinMarginTrading: Margin Order and Debit Management — KucoinMarginTrading
text: symbol Character; trading pair short (e.g., "BTC-USDT"). borrow sell base currency (BTC example). size Numeric NULL; quantity base asset short. market orders, specify either size (base qty) funds (quote qty), . funds Numeric NULL; amount quote currency receive short sale. market orders; mutually exclusive size. type Character; "limit" "market" (default "market"). price Numeric NULL; required limit orders. price sell. isIsolated Logical; TRUE isolated margin (risk limited pair), FALSE (default) cross margin (shared collateral pool). clientOid Character NULL; unique order ID (max 40 chars). Auto-generated provided (required KuCoin margin orders). stp Character NULL; self-trade prevention: "CN", "CO", "CB", "DC". remark Character NULL; order remark (max 20 ASCII chars). timeInForce Character NULL; "GTC", "GTT", "IOC", "FOK". cancelAfter Numeric NULL; auto-cancel seconds (requires timeInForce = "GTT"). postOnly Logical NULL; TRUE, order rejected match immediately. hidden Logical NULL; TRUE, order hidden order book. iceberg Logical NULL; TRUE, visibleSize shown. visibleSize Numeric NULL; visible quantity iceberg orders. dry_run Logical; TRUE, validates order without actually placing . Useful testing parameters. Default FALSE.

Open short: borrow BTC and sell it immediately order <- margin$open_short(symbol = "BTC-USDT", size = 0.001) print(order)  # 2. Close short: buy back BTC and repay the loan order <- margin$close_short(symbol = "BTC-USDT", size = 0.001) print(order)  # --- Leveraged long workflow --- # 1. Open long: borrow USDT and buy BTC with it order <- margin$open_long(symbol = "BTC-USDT", size = 0.001) print(order)  # 2. Close long: sell BTC and repay borrowed USDT order <- margin$close_long(symbol = "BTC-USDT", size = 0.001) print(order)  # --- Manual borrow/repay --- loan <- margin$borrow(currency = "USDT", size = 100) margin$repay(currency = "USDT", size = 100)  # --- Check rates before trading --- rates <- margin$get_borrow_rate(query = list(currency = "BTC,USDT")) print(rates) } # }   ## what: KucoinMarketData: Spot Market Data Retrieval — KucoinMarketData
title: KucoinMarketData: Spot Market Data Retrieval — KucoinMarketData
text: Provides methods retrieving market data KuCoin's Spot trading API, including announcements, klines, currencies, symbols, tickers, orderbooks, trade history, 24-hour statistics. Inherits KucoinBase. methods support synchronous asynchronous execution depending async parameter construction.

title: KucoinMarketData: Spot Market Data Retrieval — KucoinMarketData
text: Announcements: Fetch paginated KuCoin platform announcements filtered type, language, date range. Currencies: Retrieve metadata individual listed currencies, including chain-specific deposit/withdrawal details. Symbols: Retrieve trading pair metadata including precision, size limits, fee rates, trading status. Tickers: Access real-time Level 1 best bid/ask data individual symbols pairs. Order Books: Get partial (20/100 levels) full depth order book snapshots. Trade History: Retrieve recent 100 trades symbol. 24hr Statistics: Get rolling 24-hour market statistics (OHLCV, change rate, fees). Market List: Discover available market segments (e.g., USDS, DeFi, Meme). Klines: Fetch historical candlestick data automatic time-range segmentation bypass 1500-candle-per-request limit.

title: KucoinMarketData: Spot Market Data Retrieval — KucoinMarketData
text: methods public endpoints requiring authentication. one exception get_full_orderbook() requires valid API credentials.

title: KucoinMarketData: Spot Market Data Retrieval — KucoinMarketData
text: query Named list; filter parameters: annType (character): Announcement type filter (e.g., "latest-announcements", "activities", "new-listings", "product-updates"). lang (character): Language code (e.g., "en_US", "zh_CN"). startTime (integer): Start timestamp milliseconds. endTime (integer): End timestamp milliseconds. page_size Integer; results per page (default 50, max 100). max_pages Numeric; max pages fetch (default Inf ).

title: KucoinMarketData: Spot Market Data Retrieval — KucoinMarketData
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) columns: ann_id (integer): Announcement identifier. ann_title (character): Announcement title. ann_type (character): ;-separated category tags announcement. KuCoin returns annType array; parser collapses single character column via shared collapse_string_array_fields() helper (Treatment — matches cross-package convention used alpaca/binance plain-string arrays). Filter grepl("latest-announcements", ann_type, fixed = TRUE); recover vector via strsplit(ann_type, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]. NA_character_ KuCoin returned tags. ann_desc (character): Short description text. c_time (POSIXct): Creation datetime (coerced epoch milliseconds). language (character): Language code. ann_url (character): Full URL announcement page.

Must match BASE-QUOTE format validated verify_symbol(). side Character; order side, one "buy" "sell". price Character; limit order price take-profit leg. Must align symbol's priceIncrement. size Character; order quantity base currency (e.g., "0.0001" BTC). Must align symbol's baseIncrement. stopPrice Character; trigger price stop-limit leg. market reaches price, stop-limit order activated. limitPrice Character; limit price stop-limit leg stop triggered. price stop-limit order placed. clientOid Character NULL; unique client-assigned order identifier (max 40 characters). Useful idempotent order placement tracking. remark Character NULL; order remarks notes (max 20 characters). tradeType Character; trade type, defaults "TRADE" spot trading.

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders
text: Provides methods managing stop orders KuCoin Spot. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders
text: Stop Order Placement: Place limit market stop orders configurable trigger prices, self-trade prevention, time--force policies, iceberg/hidden order support. Order Cancellation: Cancel individual stop orders KuCoin order ID, client-assigned OID, batch-cancel stop orders matching filter criteria. Order Queries: Retrieve details individual stop orders order ID client OID, list stop orders pagination filtering support.

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders
text: methods require authentication (valid API key, secret, passphrase). Stop orders remain dormant market price crosses stop_price threshold. triggered, stop order becomes regular limit market order submitted matching engine. Use stop orders stop-loss, take-profit, breakout strategies.
code: # Synchronous usage stop <- KucoinStopOrders$new() orders <- stop$get_order_list(query = list(symbol = "BTC-USDT")) print(orders)  # Asynchronous usage stop_async <- KucoinStopOrders$new(async = TRUE) main <- coro::async(function() {   orders <- await(stop_async$get_order_list(query = list(symbol = "BTC-USDT")))   print(orders) }) main() while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now()

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders
text: Use stp parameter control behaviour orders match : "CN" (Cancel Newest): Cancel incoming order. "CO" (Cancel Oldest): Cancel resting order. "CB" (Cancel ): Cancel orders. "DC" (Decrement Cancel): Reduce quantities.

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders

title: KucoinStopOrders: Stop Order Management — KucoinStopOrders
text: type Character; order type, one "limit" "market". Determines additional parameters required. symbol Character; trading pair symbol (e.g., "BTC-USDT"). Must match BASE-QUOTE format validated verify_symbol(). side Character; order side, one "buy" "sell". stopPrice Character; trigger price stop order activates. last traded price reaches value, order placed. clientOid Character NULL; optional client-assigned unique identifier order (max 40 characters). Useful tracking orders automated systems. price Character NULL; limit order price. Required limit stop orders; must set market stop orders. align symbol's priceIncrement. size Character NULL; order quantity base currency. Required limit stop orders. market stop orders, mutually exclusive funds. funds Character NULL; order amount quote currency market stop orders. Mutually exclusive size. applicable limit stop orders. stp Character NULL; self-trade prevention strategy. One "DC" (Decrement Cancel), "CO" (Cancel Oldest), "CN" (Cancel Newest), "CB" (Cancel ). remark Character NULL; order remarks notes (max 20 ASCII characters). timeInForce Character NULL; time--force policy triggered order. One "GTC" (Good Till Cancelled), "GTT" (Good Till Time), "IOC" (Immediate Cancel), "FOK" (Fill Kill). cancelAfter Numeric NULL; number seconds auto-cancel. valid timeInForce = "GTT". postOnly Logical NULL; TRUE, triggered order rejected immediately match (guarantees maker fee). hidden Logical NULL; TRUE, triggered order hidden order book. iceberg Logical NULL; TRUE, visibleSize order displayed. visibleSize Character NULL; visible portion iceberg order. applicable iceberg = TRUE. tradeType Character; trade type, defaults "TRADE" spot trading.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: Provides methods placing, cancelling, querying spot orders KuCoin. order operations use HF (High-Frequency) trading endpoints. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: Order Placement: Place single batch limit/market orders full parameter control. Order Testing: Validate order parameters without execution via test endpoint. Order Cancellation: Cancel order ID, client OID, partially, symbol, . Order Queries: Retrieve order details, open orders, closed orders, fills, symbols activity.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: methods require authentication (valid API key, secret, passphrase). KucoinTrading class consolidates functionality previously split across KucoinSpotAddOrder, KucoinSpotCancelOrder, KucoinSpotGetOrder.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: KuCoin Spot Trading Orders

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: Limit Orders require price size. Optional: timeInForce, cancelAfter, postOnly, hidden, iceberg, visibleSize. Market Orders require either size (base currency qty) funds (quote currency qty), . price must specified.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: Use stp parameter control behaviour orders match : "CN" (Cancel Newest): Cancel incoming order. "CO" (Cancel Oldest): Cancel resting order. "CB" (Cancel ): Cancel orders. "DC" (Decrement Cancel): Reduce quantities.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: "GTC" (Good Till Cancelled): Remains filled cancelled. Default. "GTT" (Good Till Time): Cancels cancelAfter seconds. "IOC" (Immediate Cancel): Fill immediately cancel remainder. "FOK" (Fill Kill): Fill entirely cancel completely.

code: KucoinTrading$add_order(   type,   symbol,   side,   clientOid = NULL,   price = NULL,   size = NULL,   funds = NULL,   stp = NULL,   tags = NULL,   remark = NULL,   timeInForce = NULL,   cancelAfter = NULL,   postOnly = NULL,   hidden = NULL,   iceberg = NULL,   visibleSize = NULL )

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: type Character; "limit" "market". symbol Character; trading pair (e.g., "BTC-USDT"). side Character; "buy" "sell". clientOid Character NULL; unique client order ID (max 40 chars). price Numeric NULL; price limit orders. Must align priceIncrement. Required limit orders; must set market orders. size Numeric NULL; quantity base currency. Must align baseIncrement. Required limit orders; optional market orders (mutually exclusive funds). funds Numeric NULL; amount quote currency market orders. Mutually exclusive size. applicable limit orders. stp Character NULL; self-trade prevention: "CN", "CO", "CB", "DC". tags Character NULL; order tag (max 20 ASCII chars). remark Character NULL; remarks (max 20 ASCII chars). timeInForce Character NULL; "GTC", "GTT", "IOC", "FOK". cancelAfter Numeric NULL; auto-cancel seconds (requires timeInForce = "GTT"). postOnly Logical NULL; TRUE, order rejected match immediately. hidden Logical NULL; TRUE, order hidden order book. iceberg Logical NULL; TRUE, visibleSize shown. visibleSize Numeric NULL; visible quantity iceberg orders.

title: KucoinTrading: Spot Order Management — KucoinTrading
text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) columns: order_id (character): KuCoin-assigned order identifier. client_oid (character): Client-provided order identifier.

title: KucoinTransfer: Internal Transfer Management — KucoinTransfer
text: Provides methods transferring funds KuCoin accounts (main, trade, margin, etc.) master sub-accounts. Inherits KucoinBase.

title: KucoinTransfer: Internal Transfer Management — KucoinTransfer
text: Fund Movement: Transfer funds account types (e.g., main → trade) deposited funds can used HF spot trading. Sub-Account Funding: Move funds master sub-accounts. Balance Queries: Check much currency available transfer specific account type.

title: KucoinTransfer: Internal Transfer Management — KucoinTransfer
text: methods require authentication (valid API key, secret, passphrase). API key must FlexTransfers (universal transfer) permission add_transfer(). get_transferable() method requires General permission.
code: # Synchronous usage transfer <- KucoinTransfer$new() balance <- transfer$get_transferable(currency = "USDT", type = "MAIN")  # Asynchronous usage transfer_async <- KucoinTransfer$new(async = TRUE) coro::async(function() {   balance <- await(transfer_async$get_transferable(currency = "USDT", type = "MAIN"))   print(balance) })()

title: KucoinTransfer: Internal Transfer Management — KucoinTransfer
text: KuCoin uses separate accounts different purposes: "MAIN": Funding account — deposits land default. "TRADE": Spot trading account — required HF orders. "MARGIN": Cross-margin account. "ISOLATED": Isolated-margin account (requires fromAccountTag/toAccountTag symbol). "CONTRACT": Futures account.

title: KucoinTransfer: Internal Transfer Management — KucoinTransfer
text: "INTERNAL": accounts (e.g., MAIN → TRADE). "PARENT_TO_SUB": master sub-account. "SUB_TO_PARENT": sub-account master.

title: KucoinTransfer: Internal Transfer Management — KucoinTransfer
text: clientOid Character; unique client order ID idempotency (max 128 bits, e.g., UUID). currency Character; currency code (e.g., "BTC", "USDT"). amount Character; transfer amount (positive, multiple currency precision). type Character; transfer type: "INTERNAL", "PARENT_TO_SUB", "SUB_TO_PARENT". fromAccountType Character; source account type: "MAIN", "TRADE", "CONTRACT", "MARGIN", "ISOLATED", "MARGIN_V2", "ISOLATED_V2". toAccountType Character; destination account type (options fromAccountType). fromUserId Character NULL; source user ID (required "SUB_TO_PARENT" transfers). fromAccountTag Character NULL; symbol ISOLATED/ISOLATED_V2 source accounts (e.g., "BTC-USDT"). toUserId Character NULL; destination user ID (required "PARENT_TO_SUB" transfers). toAccountTag Character NULL; symbol ISOLATED/ISOLATED_V2 destination accounts (e.g., "BTC-USDT").

text: data.table (promise<data.table> constructed async = TRUE) one row columns: order_id (character): transfer order identifier.

Quota Queries: Check withdrawal limits, minimum fees, available balances per currency/chain. History Retrieval: Retrieve paginated withdrawal records status tracking timestamps. Detail Lookup: Get comprehensive details specific withdrawal ID.

title: Backfill KuCoin Kline Data to CSV — kucoin_backfill_klines
text: symbols Character vector trading pair symbols (e.g., c("BTC-USDT", "ETH-USDT")). Must NULL empty. timeframes Character vector candle timeframes (e.g., c("1day", "1hour")). Valid values names internal timeframe map: "1min", "3min", "5min", "15min", "30min", "1hour", "2hour", "4hour", "6hour", "8hour", "12hour", "1day", "1week", "1month". POSIXct numeric; start backfill window. Defaults one year ago. Values "2017-01-01" (-Inf) clamped "2017-01-01" since KuCoin data exist date. POSIXct numeric; end backfill window. Defaults current time. Inf replaced current time. file Character; path output CSV file. Data appended incrementally progress saved even process interrupted. base_url Character; KuCoin API base URL. sleep Numeric; seconds sleep symbol-timeframe combination respect rate limits. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages via rlang::inform().
